bidding.note: Unbalanced 0=3=5=5 (void S), 13 HCP: open higher of two 5-card minors 1D, rebid 2C shows the second suit cheaply, no reverse. Clean unbalanced two-suiter rebid.

Balanced 4=3=2=4, 19 HCP should JUMP to 2NT after 1S response; instead South jump-raised to 4S (4-card support) into Blackwood, reaching an odd 5S. Never shows the 18-19 balanced 2NT rebid the lesson teaches.
Balanced 3=4=3=3, 19 HCP: open 1C, jump to 2NT over 1NT response = textbook 18-19 balanced rebid; partner raises to 3N. Ideal.
Balanced 2=3=3=5, 14 HCP: open 1C, rebid 1NT over 1D response = textbook 12-14 balanced rebid. Deep inside the rule; ideal first board.
Balanced 3=3=5=2, 18 HCP: open 1D, jump to 2NT over 1S = correct 18-19 balanced rebid; later 4S is partner-driven after 3H. Rebid is clean.
Balanced 4=3=3=3, 18 HCP: open 1C, jump to 2NT over 1D = correct 18-19 balanced rebid; partner drives to 4S. Clean.
Balanced 3=2=4=4, 12 HCP: open 1D, rebid 1NT over 1H = textbook 12-14 balanced rebid. Deep inside the rule.
Balanced 3=3=4=3, 18 HCP (flags mislabel; no void): open 1D, jump 2NT over 1S = correct 18-19 balanced rebid; 3C checkback to 3N. Clean.
Unbalanced 2=1=4=6, 17 HCP: open longest 1C, rebid 2D. 2D is a REVERSE but sound with 17 and 6-4; a wrinkle for beginners (extras + higher second suit). Partner jumps to 3N.
Balanced 3=3=3=4, 14 HCP (data HCP wrong; hand is 14): open 1C, rebid 1NT over 1H = textbook 12-14 balanced rebid. Ideal.
Unbalanced 3=6=3=1, 13 HCP: open longest 1H, rebid 2H over 1S = simple suit rebid showing a 6-card suit, minimum. Clean single-suiter rebid.
Balanced 3=4=4=2, 18 HCP (flags mislabel; no void): open 1D, jump 2NT over 1NT = correct 18-19 balanced rebid; partner raises to 3N. Clean.
5=2=3=3, 18 HCP, open 1S; partner raises to 2S so South accepts game with 4S. With a fit and 18 there is no NT rebid to make - bidding game is the clean call.
Unbalanced 1=7=2=3, 17 HCP: open 1H, jump to 4H over 1S showing a huge single-suiter (~8 tricks). Sound but aggressive and skips the simple 2H rebid; DD says it is not cold.
Unbalanced 5=1=4=3, 17 HCP: open longest 1S, rebid 2D over 1NT = natural second suit, cheap (not a reverse). Clean unbalanced two-suiter rebid.
Unbalanced 4=6=2=1, 13 HCP: open longest 1H, rebid 2H over 2C = shows the 6-card suit, minimum; 3H later confirms. Clean single-suiter rebid (2S also possible).
Balanced 3=3=5=2, 19 HCP: open 1D, jump 2NT over 1H = textbook 18-19 balanced rebid; 3C checkback to 3N. Clean.
Unbalanced 5=4=3=1, 16 HCP: open higher of two long suits 1S, rebid 2H over 1NT = natural second suit, cheap (no reverse). Clean two-suiter rebid.

3=5=2=3, 14 HCP minimum: open 1H, rebid 2H over 2D showing 5+ hearts, minimum; then 3N over partner's 2NT. Sound but slightly awkward - balanced 14 with only a 5-card suit; the simple 2H is the safe rebid.

Robot misbid: South 4=3=1=5, 17 HCP with a SINGLETON diamond rebid 1NT over partner's 1D - notrump rebid with a stiff in partner's suit, and 17 is above the 12-14 1NT range. Correct rebid is 1S (4-card spade). Unusable.

Unbalanced 6=4=2=1, 17 HCP: open longest 1S, jump-shift 3H over 1NT showing a strong 6-4; partner corrects to 3N. Correct for 17 but the jump is advanced; a plain 2H second-suit rebid is the beginner version.
Balanced 18 (3-3-3-4): open 1C, jump-rebid 2NT — model 18-19 balanced rebid; North raises to sound 3N.
Unbalanced 5-4 (3-5-1-4) 18 HCP: opens longest 1H then jump-shifts 3C into second suit — right second-suit idea but a strong jump; reaches 3N.
Unbalanced one-suiter (1-6-3-3) 16 HCP: open 1H, jump-rebid 3H showing 6+ and extras; North raises to a good 4H.
Balanced 18 (4-3-4-2): open 1D, jump-rebid 2NT; 4-4 spade fit surfaces and pair settles in a sound 4S.
Balanced 12-14 (4-3-2-4): rebid 1S to show the spade suit instead of the lesson's 1NT — defensible up-the-line style but off the balanced-1NT teaching point.
Partner jump-raises 1H to 3H, so South's 4H is a plain game acceptance, not a hand-type-driven rebid — the prepared-rebid decision never happens.
Balanced 12-14 (2-3-3-5, 5 clubs) 13 HCP: open 1C, rebid 1NT — exactly the balanced-minimum rule.
Unbalanced 5-4 (5-4-3-1) 13 HCP: open higher-ranking 1S, rebid 2H (no reverse) — model minimum two-suiter; North gives 2S preference.
Robot misbid: balanced 19 (4-4-2-3) jumps to 4H then drives to 6H, which is off double-dummy — not the prepared 2NT/3NT rebid and a silly slam.
Balanced 19 (3-3-3-4): open 1C, jump-rebid 2NT; 3N is the normal 30-HCP contract (fails only on a bad double-dummy layout).
Balanced 19 (2-4-4-3): open 1D, jump-rebid 2NT; North raises to 3N, made comfortably.
Balanced 19 (4-3-3-3): open 1C, jump-rebid 2NT; 3N is the right shot on 26 HCP (unlucky DD miss).
Balanced 12-14 (3-2-4-4) 13 HCP: open 1D, rebid 1NT — clean balanced-minimum rebid.
Balanced 19 (5-3-2-3): open five-card 1S, jump-rebid 2NT; North raises to 3N — model 18-19 balanced rebid.
Balanced 14 (3-4-3-3) with 4 hearts: raises partner's 1H to 2H showing the 4-4 fit rather than rebidding 1NT — correct fit-first bridge, minor departure from the pattern.
Unbalanced 5-5 (1-5-5-2) 12 HCP: open higher 1H, rebid 2D (no reverse) — model two-suiter; North leaps to 4S on a seven-bagger.
Unbalanced 5-4 (4-3-5-1) 16 HCP: rebid 2S over 1NT is a reverse on borderline strength; a good 5-card suit makes ~17 total, so strong players could disagree with the reverse.
Balanced 12-14 (3-3-4-3) 14 HCP: open 1D, rebid 1NT — clean balanced-minimum rebid.
Balanced 18 (3-4-3-3): after partner's 2C club raise, South shows a stopper with 2H and the pair lands in 2NT (right spot on 25 HCP) — sound but an advanced sequence, not the simple rebid pattern.
Balanced 12-14 (3-5-3-2) 14 HCP: open five-card 1H, rebid 1NT over 1S — model balanced-minimum rebid with a five-card major.
Balanced 14 (2-3-4-4) with 4-4 minors: rebids 2C to show the second minor rather than 1NT — defensible either way; pair rests in a sound 2C part-score.
Robot misbid: 3NT rebid with a VOID in diamonds after partner jumped in diamonds; should support diamonds or show the 5-5 club suit, never bid 3NT with a void.
Balanced 19 HCP (2-5-3-3): open 1H, jump to 2NT, then support partner's 6-card spades to 4S. Model balanced-jump rebid.
Balanced 18 HCP (4-3-3-3): open 1C, jump to 2NT showing the balanced 18-19; partner explores and settles in 3NT.
Balanced 18 HCP (3-2-4-4): open 1D (4-4 minors), jump to 2NT; partner raises to 3NT. Clean balanced jump.
Unbalanced 18 HCP (5-3-1-4): open 1S, rebid second suit clubs; the JUMP to 3C shows the 5-4 shape plus extras rather than a bare minimum rebid.
13 HCP with one 5-card suit (3-2-5-3): open 1D and rebid 2D over partner's 2C, showing the 5th diamond with no side suit to bid.
Unbalanced 16 HCP (5-1-4-3): open 1S; partner raises, so the 'rebid' becomes a game-try 3S invite with the fit rather than a new-suit rebid.
Unbalanced 18 HCP (1-3-5-4): open 1D, jump-rebid 3C showing the 5-4 second suit plus extras; partner corrects to 3NT.
Balanced 12 HCP (3-4-2-4): open 1C, rebid 1NT over partner's 1D. Model minimum-balanced 1NT rebid.
Balanced 18 HCP (4-3-3-3): open 1C, jump to 2NT; partner explores and lands in 3NT. Clean balanced-jump rebid.
Balanced 12 HCP (4-2-4-3): after 1D-1H, showing the 4-card spade major with 1S is correct and preferred to a 1NT rebid; don't bypass a 4-card major.
13 HCP with one 5-card suit (3-5-2-3): open 1H, rebid 2H over partner's 2C showing the 5th heart with no side 4-card suit.
Unbalanced 17 HCP (5-3-1-4): open 1S, rebid second suit 2C naturally (non-reverse); partner drives to 3NT.
Unbalanced 13 HCP (3-5-4-1): open 1H, rebid second suit 2D over 1NT (non-reverse, minimum-safe). Model second-suit rebid.
Unbalanced 13 HCP (5-3-1-4): open 1S, rebid second suit 2C over 1NT (non-reverse). Clean prepared second-suit rebid.
Balanced 19 HCP (3-3-3-4): open 1C, jump to 2NT over partner's 1H. Model balanced 18-19 jump rebid.

Unbalanced 16 HCP (4-3-5-1): open 1D, and 2S over 2C is technically a reverse on a 16-count; defensible in a game-forcing 2/1 (2S just shows shape) but borderline for the no-reverse-on-minimum rule; rebidding 3D avoids it.
18 HCP with a 5-card major (5-2-3-3): open 1S; partner raises so the 'rebid' is a straightforward jump to game 4S rather than a NT choice.
Unbalanced 18 HCP (6-3-3-1): open 1S; partner raises so the rebid is a clear jump to game 4S with the six-card suit and fit.
Unbalanced 17 HCP (3-1-5-4): open 1D, rebid second suit 2C over 1S (non-reverse). Model prepared second-suit rebid.
Balanced 12 HCP (3-3-4-3): open 1D, rebid 1NT over partner's 1S. Model minimum-balanced 1NT rebid.
Balanced 18 (3433): after 1NT response, jump to 2NT shows the 18-19 balanced hand exactly.
Balanced 14 (5332): open 1C, rebid 1NT — the 12-14 balanced rule, clean.
Balanced 19 (3343): open 1D, jump to 2NT — the 18-19 balanced rule.
Unbalanced 18 (3-4-5-1): open longest 1D, reverse to 2H showing the strong 5-4 — extras justify the reverse.
Balanced 18 (3235): open 1C, jump to 2NT over the 1NT response — 18-19 balanced rule.
Balanced 14 (4342): 1D-1H-1S shows the 4-card spade up the line vs the rule's 1NT rebid; both sound with a doubleton club.
Balanced 13 (2443): open 1D, rebid 1NT — the 12-14 balanced rule.
Balanced 14 (4243): 1D-1H-1S shows the 4-card spade up the line vs the rule's 1NT; both fine with a doubleton heart.
Balanced 14 with 4-card heart support: raise partner's 1H to 2H — showing the fit beats a NT rebid.
Balanced 14 (4234): 1C-1D-1NT per the rule; 1S to show the 4-card spade is the minor wrinkle.
Balanced 19 (3334): open 1C, jump to 2NT — the 18-19 balanced rule.
Balanced 19 (4324): open 1C, jump to 2NT — 18-19 balanced rule; later 3S is just cue toward 3NT.
Balanced 19 (3325): open 1C, jump to 2NT — 18-19 balanced rule.
Unbalanced (3-1-5-4): open longest 1D, rebid the 4-card club (lower-ranking, no reverse) — natural minimum.
Balanced 12 (3325): open 1C, rebid 1NT — the 12-14 balanced rule.
Unbalanced 15 with 6 diamonds: jump-rebid 3D shows the strong 6-card suit and extras; showing the 4-card heart with 2H also playable.
Balanced 18 (2335): open 1C, jump to 2NT — 18-19 balanced rule (the 'two-suiter' flag is really a 5332).
Balanced 12 (3235): open 1C, rebid 1NT after 1S — the 12-14 balanced rule.
Balanced 12 (3334): open 1C, rebid 1NT after 1S — the 12-14 balanced rule.
Balanced 12 (4333): 1C-1H-1S shows the 4-card spade up the line vs the rule's 1NT rebid; both defensible.
Unbalanced (3-1-3-6): open longest 1C, rebid 2C on the 6-card suit — natural minimum, no second suit worth showing.
12-14 balanced 5-3-3-2 (5 clubs): open 1C, rebid 1NT — the balanced-minimum rebid.
Unbalanced 5-4 (spade singleton): open longest 1H, rebid second suit 2D — cheap, non-reverse.
18-count balanced 4-3-4-2 opens 1D (too strong for 1NT); over partner's limit 3D the value bid is 3N.
18-19 balanced: open 1C, JUMP to 2NT — exactly the strong-balanced rebid rule.
Robot misbid: 12-count balanced 4-3-4-2 reverses into 2S over 2C — should rebid 2NT/2D, never reverse on a minimum.
Two 5-card suits (D/C), spade singleton: open the higher 1D, rebid the lower 2C cheaply.
19-count balanced: open 1D, JUMP to 2NT — top-of-range strong-balanced rebid.
1S opener (13, 5-3-3-2); partner's 2H is game-forcing, so raising 3-card support to 4H names the strain — 3H is the quieter route.
Minimum unbalanced with 6 diamonds: open 1D, rebid 2D — repeat the long suit.
18 balanced: open 1C, JUMP to 2NT over 1S — strong-balanced rebid rule.
Unbalanced 5-4 (club singleton): open 1D, rebid the 4-card heart second suit 2H naturally (GF already set).
18 HCP with 5 hearts opposite a single raise: bid the game 4H — the value call.
18 balanced 3-2-4-4: open 1D, JUMP to 2NT — strong-balanced rebid rule.
18 balanced with 4-card spade support: raising partner's major to 4S beats the 2NT jump once the fit shows — a fit-jump judgment.
19-count balanced (void-flag spurious; hand is 4-3-2-4): open 1C, JUMP to 2NT.
13 HCP with 6 hearts opposite an invitational 3H: accept and bid 4H.
Robot distorted jump: 1C-1D-5D leaps past the natural 2D/3D rebid on 5-4 minors — an absurd, exploration-skipping bid.
16 HCP with 6 hearts opposite invitational 3H: accept, bid 4H.
18 balanced 4-3-3-3: open 1C, JUMP to 2NT — strong-balanced rebid rule (fit found later).
18-count 5-3-3-2 opens 1S; over GF 2H (no heart fit) 3N is the natural landing — minor wrinkle: diamond stopper is thin.
18 balanced 2-3-4-4: open 1D, JUMP to 2NT — strong-balanced rebid rule.
Bal 18 4333 with 4-card spade support: jump-raise 3S over North's 1S is standard; 2NT jump is the alt but the fit rates the raise.
Bal 18 (2-4-3-4), no fit for 1S: jump to 2NT shows 18-19 balanced. Textbook.
12 HCP semi-bal 3-3-2-5, no 4-card major: rebid 1NT shows 12-14. Clean.
18 HCP 3-2-4-4; North jumped to 3D (limit raise), South accepts with 3NT. Sound; North's jump preempts the tidy 1NT rebid.
Bal 13 (2-4-4-3) with 4-card heart support: raise North's 1H to 2H. Textbook fit raise; 'void' flag spurious.
Unbalanced 6-4 (spades-clubs), heart void: open longer 1S, rebid clubs 3C naturally. Textbook two-suiter.
4-5 majors, singleton club, 15 HCP: open longer 1H, show spades with 3S over North's 3C. Sound shape-showing; 3NT contract is North's call.
Bal 19 (3-2-5-3): jump to 2NT shows 18-19. Textbook (5-card diamond but balanced).
Bal 18 5-3-3-2: open 1S, jump 2NT shows 18-19 balanced. Textbook; 'two-suiter' flag spurious.
Bal 19 5-2-3-3: open 1S, jump to 2NT shows 18-19. Textbook.
Bal 14 (2-4-3-4): rebid 1NT shows 12-14. Textbook.
5-5 spades-diamonds, 16 HCP: open higher 1S, rebid diamonds cheaply (no reverse). Textbook two-suiter.
Bal 14 3-3-3-4: rebid 1NT shows 12-14. Textbook.
Bal 14 (2-4-3-4): after 1C-1D rebid 1NT (can't show hearts w/o reverse). Textbook.
Unbalanced 6-4 spades-clubs: open longer 1S, rebid clubs 2C naturally. Textbook two-suiter.
5-4 clubs-diamonds, 17 HCP: open longer 1C, then 2D is a reverse showing both suits + extras — 17 is ample. Sound; a reverse rather than the 'cheap rebid' ideal.
5-4 spades-diamonds, singleton heart, 14 HCP: open longer 1S, rebid diamonds 2D (not a reverse). Textbook two-suiter.
Bal 12 (2-4-4-3): after 1D-1S rebid 1NT (12-14). Textbook.
Bal 18 (3-3-5-2): open 1D, jump to 2NT shows 18-19. Textbook.
14 HCP 3-5-2-3: rebid 1NT (12-14) is textbook; South's later leap to 3NT over 2D is an overbid on 14.
5-5 hearts-clubs, singleton spade, 17 HCP: open higher 1H, rebid clubs 2C cheaply (no reverse). Textbook two-suiter.
Unbalanced 5=4=1=3; open longer spades, rebid 2H (2nd suit, below spades so no reverse) — cheap two-suiter rebid.
1=4=6=2 16 HCP; instead of rebidding the 6-card diamond, South jump-raises partner's 1H to 3H on the found 4-card major fit — sound; 3D rebid also fine.
Unbalanced 1=6=4=2; open longer hearts, rebid 2D (2nd suit, below hearts, no reverse); partner gives heart preference.
Unbalanced 1=5=4=3, correct natural 2D rebid (2nd suit, no reverse); 17 HCP is strong to be left in 2D but the pattern is right and partner passed.
Balanced-ish 3=3=5=2 12 HCP; partner's 1NT response pre-empts the 1NT rebid, so natural minimum 2D shows the 5th diamond; North raises to 3D.
Balanced 4432 19 HCP — jump to 2NT to show 18-19; North raises 3NT.
Balanced 5332 18 HCP — jump to 2NT (18-19); North bids 3NT.
Balanced 4333 19 HCP — jump to 2NT (18-19); North raises 3NT.
Balanced 4432 13 HCP — rebid 1NT over 1H (12-14); two-suiter flag misleading, hand is flat.
Balanced 5332 18 HCP with 5-card major — open 1H, jump 2NT to show 18-19; North checks back 3C, 3NT reached. DD failure is card luck.
Balanced 4432 14 HCP — after 1C-1S, rebid 1NT (12-14); North passes with 6.
Balanced 5332 19 HCP with 5-card major — open 1H, jump 2NT (18-19); North raises 3NT.
Balanced 4432 18 HCP — jump 2NT (18-19); North bids 3NT. DD failure is card luck.
Balanced 4432 18 HCP — jump 2NT (18-19); North checks back 3C, 3NT reached.
Balanced 4333 14 HCP — after 1C-1D, rebid 1NT (12-14); North drives to 3NT.
Balanced 5332 14 HCP — after 1C-1D, rebid 1NT (12-14); North passes, correct stop at 22 combined.
Balanced 4432 19 HCP — jump 2NT (18-19); North shows a 6-card heart suit (3H), South places 4H in North's long major.
Balanced 4432 19 HCP — open 1C, jump 2NT (18-19); North shows spades (3S), 4S in the 4-4 fit. DD failure is card luck.

Robot misbid: unbalanced 4=3=1=5 with a singleton diamond rebid 1NT over partner's 1D; correct rebid is 1S (show the 4-card spade, keep the 5-4 pattern). NT rebid with a stiff breaks the hand-type rule.
Balanced 4432 12 HCP — rebid 1NT (12-14) then decline North's 2NT invite with the minimum; two-suiter/void flags are spurious (no void present).
Balanced 4432 13 HCP — rebid 1NT (12-14); after North's 2C South bids 2NT. DD failure is card luck.
14 HCP balanced 5332; open 1D, rebid 1NT (12-14) is the textbook plan-your-rebid call.
12 HCP balanced 5332 (5 clubs); open 1C, rebid 1NT is textbook; North's thin 3N drive is North's, not South's rebid.
14 HCP balanced 4333; open 1C, rebid 1NT (12-14) is the textbook rebid.
14 HCP balanced 4333; open 1C, rebid 1NT (12-14), passed out 1NT.
14 HCP balanced 5332; open longest suit 1H, rebid 1NT (12-14) is textbook.
14 HCP balanced 4333 (4 diamonds); open 1D, rebid 1NT (12-14) is textbook.
13 HCP balanced 5332 (5 clubs); open 1C, rebid 1NT (12-14), passed out 1NT=.
13 HCP balanced 4-4-3-2; open the minor 1D (4-4 minors), rebid 1NT (12-14) is textbook.
13 HCP balanced 4333; open 1D, rebid 1NT (12-14) is the textbook rebid.
14 HCP balanced 4-4-3-2; open 1D, rebid 1NT (12-14) shows the balanced minimum after 1S.
Robot misbid: unbalanced 5-4-3-1 with singleton heart K opposite North's 1H; must rebid 2D (second suit), not 1NT with a stiff.
14 HCP balanced 5332 (5 diamonds); open 1D, rebid 1NT (12-14) is textbook.
12 HCP balanced 5332 (5 clubs); open 1C, rebid 1NT (12-14), passed out 1NT.
13 HCP balanced 4333; open 1C, rebid 1NT (12-14) is textbook (void/two-suiter flags are other seats, not South).
13 HCP balanced 4-4-3-2; open 1D, rebid 1NT (12-14) textbook; South's rebid is the hero call despite the extended auction.
